skill.name: handoff
skill.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
skill.summary: Creates a summary of the current conversation so another AI agent can continue the work without losing context.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 4 issues, but 3 are false positives and 1 is a low-risk legitimate pattern. The two 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (lines 3 and 13) have no basis in the file content. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' finding (line 7) is markdown code formatting for a legitimate mktemp command. The 'temp file creation' finding (line 7) is accurate but reflects standard safe behavior for a handoff skill that writes temporary documents. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious patterns were detected. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Temp file creation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI to create a temporary file using mktemp for writing a handoff document. This is standard and safe behavior — mktemp creates files in the system temp directory with secure random names. No risk of overwriting existing files or writing to sensitive locations.

content.user_title: Handoff - Seamlessly Transfer Work Between AI Agents
content.value_statement: Developers lose valuable context every time they start a new AI coding session. This skill generates a compact handoff document that summarizes the current conversation, key artifacts, and next steps so a fresh agent can pick up the work immediately.
actual_capabilities: Generates a structured handoff document summarizing the current conversation
References existing artifacts by path or URL to avoid content duplication
Suggests relevant skills for the next session to continue the work
Tailors the handoff document based on user-provided focus arguments
Saves the document to a temporary file for easy access by the next agent
limitations: Cannot transfer in-memory state or uncommitted file changes between sessions
Requires the next agent to have access to the same file system and repository
Handoff quality depends on the clarity and completeness of the current conversation
Does not automatically trigger the next session or agent

use_cases.title: Shift Change Between Developers
use_cases.description: A developer finishing their shift creates a handoff so a colleague on a different schedule can continue the work without needing a full re-read of the conversation history.
use_cases.target_user: Team lead or developer working in shifts

use_cases.title: Context Switch Recovery
use_cases.description: A developer pauses work on one feature to handle an urgent bug fix. The handoff skill preserves the feature context so it can be resumed later without relearning the details.

use_cases.title: Onboarding a New Team Member
use_cases.description: A senior developer creates a handoff document that summarizes decisions, open questions, and next steps so a junior developer can pick up the task independently.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Handoff Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a handoff document for the current session.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick handoff with no special instructions

prompt_templates.title: Handoff with Context Arguments
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a handoff document. The next session will focus on implementing the Stripe payment webhook handler.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handoff with a specific focus area for the next agent

prompt_templates.title: Handoff with Skill Recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a handoff document for the current refactoring work. Suggest which skills the next session should use.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handoff that includes tool and skill recommendations

prompt_templates.title: Handoff Referencing External Artifacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a handoff document. Reference the PRD at docs/auth-prd.md and the ADR at docs/adr-001.md instead of duplicating their content.
prompt_templates.target_user: Advanced user managing multiple project artifacts

output_examples.input: Generate a handoff for the payment API integration work.
output: - Stripe API connection is configured and tested
- Database schema for payments is defined (see PRD-42)
- Refund endpoint has failing integration tests
Fix the refund endpoint tests and add idempotency key support.

output_examples.input: Create a handoff for the frontend authentication refactoring.
output: - Login and registration components are extracted from App.tsx
- OAuth callback handler has a bug in state parameter validation
- Session token refresh logic is not implemented
Fix OAuth state validation and implement token refresh.

best_practices: Provide a clear focus argument describing what the next session will work on for a more relevant handoff document.
Reference existing artifacts by path or URL to keep the handoff concise and avoid outdated duplicate content.
Review the generated handoff document before ending the session to ensure it captures all critical context.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate large artifact contents in the handoff — reference them by path or URL instead.
Do not skip the handoff for short sessions, even brief context summaries save time on resumption.
Do not include sensitive credentials or secrets in the handoff document as it is saved to a file.

faq.question: What file format does the handoff document use?
faq.answer: The handoff is saved as a Markdown file with a .md extension. It uses mktemp to create a unique file in the system temporary directory.

faq.question: Can I customize what information goes into the handoff?
faq.answer: Yes. You can pass an argument describing what the next session will focus on, and the handoff will be tailored accordingly.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all AI coding tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The handoff document is a plain Markdown file that any tool can read.

faq.question: Will the handoff document persist after the session ends?
faq.answer: Yes. The file is saved to the system temp directory and will remain there until cleaned up by the operating system or manually deleted.

faq.question: How long should a handoff document be?
faq.answer: The handoff should be as short as possible while capturing essential context. The skill avoids duplicating content already captured in artifacts like PRDs and ADRs.

faq.question: Can I pass a handoff document to another developer instead of an AI?
faq.answer: Yes. The Markdown format is human-readable and can be shared with any developer or AI agent.
